description: Feeling or showing tiredness, especially from sustained effort. 'Shorui' names the quality of weariness—the body and mind calling for rest.
sound_symbolism: The word droops with fatigue. 'Sho' /ʃo/ begins with soft exhale, energy fading. 'Ru' rolls slowly, momentum lost. Finally 'i' arrives weakly, weariness complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'shorui miona' (weary person). Acknowledges the natural need for rest—weariness is not failure but signal.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Acknowledging weariness enables care—the tired body teaches us to rest rather than push through.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Weary workers rested—traditional rhythms included time for recovery, not endless productivity.
